commit.message:
feat: switch to hanthor/changelog-action for SBOM-based changelogs

- generate-changelog-release.yml: replace custom changelogs.py with
  hanthor/changelog-action, passing explicit registry/cosign-key/images
  (yellowfin, albacore, skipjack). Stream defaults to 'gnome'.
  Adds dry_run input for safe testing before publishing releases.

- reusable-build-image.yml: also publish {flavor}-YYYYMMDD tags
  (e.g. gnome-20250323) alongside bare YYYYMMDD, so the changelog
  action's stream-based tag discovery can find dated builds by flavor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add consolidated AutoModerator reference document

Create automod-reference.md from 6 Reddit wiki source files:
- Reddit 2.txt (Writing Basic Rules)
- Reddit 4.txt (Inputs, Modifiers & Outputs)
- Reddit 5.txt (Limitations)
- Reddit 3.txt (Common Mistakes)
- Reddit 6.txt (YAML Syntax & Error Messages)
- Reddit 1.yml (Library of Common Rules)

The document includes:
- Table of contents linking to all major sections
- 60 YAML code blocks with proper fencing
- Horizontal rules between major sections
- Proper heading hierarchy (##/###/####)
- All source content preserved faithfully

commit.message:
fix: handle empty manifests in changelogs.py get_tags

When all image fetches fail (e.g. lts tag not yet published),
get_manifests returns an empty dict. get_tags then crashes with
StopIteration on next(iter({})).

Add an early exit with a clear message instead.

commit.message:
refactor: Extract identifier sets into constants

Extract hardcoded identifier string checks into reusable constant sets in parser.ts:
- EXAMPLE_IDENTIFIERS: Set(['test', 'it', 'spec', 'example'])
- DESCRIBE_IDENTIFIERS: Set(['describe', 'context', 'suite'])

Benefits:
- Single source of truth for identifier names
- Easier to maintain and extend with new aliases
- O(1) lookup performance with Set.has()
- More readable and intention-revealing code

This follows the DRY principle and makes future identifier additions straightforward.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Port tracing capabilities to Gluon IrisDeviceCtx API (#473)

Add GluonDeviceTracing device-side aggregate for Gluon kernels, mirroring
the existing Triton DeviceTracing with the same SoA buffer layout and
bounds-checked event recording. IrisDeviceCtx.initialize() gains a
tracing constexpr parameter; when False the tracing code is dead-code
eliminated (zero overhead confirmed via assembly diff). IrisGluon host
class wires up the existing Tracing manager so enable/reset/export work
identically to the Triton path.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Overhaul Post-Processing (#828)

Decouple post-processing from `SearchStrategy` so that search methods
accept an explicit post-processor parameter. This enables callers to
supply custom post-processing logic (reranking, filtering, ID
translation) without defining new strategy types.

## Search trait changes

The `Search` trait now requires `S: SearchStrategy<DP, T>` at the trait
level, and introduces two new generic parameters on the `search` method:

- `O` - the output type written to the buffer (used to be a parameter of
`SearchStrategy`)
- `PP` — the post-processor, bounded by `for<'a>
SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync`
- `OB` — the output buffer, bounded by `SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send +
?Sized`

Previously, `OB` was a trait-level generic and the post-processor was
obtained internally from the strategy. Now both are method-level
generics, giving callers control over each independently.

The output "id" type `O` has been removed from `SearchStrategy`
entirely, which greatly simplifies things.

At the `DiskANNIndex` level, two entry points are provided:

- `search()` — requires `S: DefaultPostProcessor`, creates the
strategy's default processor automatically, then delegates to
`search_with()`. This is the common path.
- `search_with()` — requires only `S: SearchStrategy`, accepts any
compatible `PP`. This is the extension point for custom post-processing.

## DefaultPostProcessor

Strategies opt in to default post-processing by implementing
`DefaultPostProcessor`:

```rust
pub trait DefaultPostProcessor<Provider, T, O>: SearchStrategy<Provider, T> {
    type Processor: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<Self::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync;
    fn default_post_processor(&self) -> Self::Processor;
}
```

The `default_post_processor!` macro covers the common case where the
processor is `Default`-constructible. `DefaultSearchStrategy` is a
convenience alias for `SearchStrategy + DefaultPostProcessor`.

## Start-point filtering

The in-mem code used to rely on a special `Internal` type to customize
post-processing for the inplace delete pipeline.
Now that post-processing is customizable, this workaround is no longer
needed.

Start-point filtering is composed at the type level using the existing
`Pipeline` mechanism:

- `HasDefaultProcessor::Processor = Pipeline<FilterStartPoints,
RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>` — filters start points during regular search
- `InplaceDeleteStrategy::SearchPostProcessor = RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy`
— no start-point filtering during delete re-pruning

## Range search

To respond to moving the output buffer to a method level geneirc, range
search now writes results directly through a `DistanceFiltered` output
buffer wrapper that applies radius and inner-radius filtering inline
during post-processing. This replaces the previous approach of
allocating intermediate `Vec`s and copying results through an
`IdDistance` buffer.

`SearchOutputBuffer` is now implemented for `Vec<Neighbor<I>>`,
providing an unbounded growable buffer suitable for range search and
other cases where the result count is not known in advance.

The `RangeSearchResults` type has been removed now that a
`SearchOutputBuffer` is properly used.

## Inplace Delete
Since the post-processor is now a little decoupled from
`SearchStrategy`, rustc is unable to fully resolve the existing bounds
for the "caching" provider. Try as I might, I could not get anything to
work without adding a `DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type to
`InplaceDeleteStrategy`. At the end of the day, I think this is a
compromise we'll have to live with.

## Migration guide

**If you implement `SearchStrategy`** — minimal changes required. Remove
the `O` parfameter (if needed). Implement `DefaultPostProcessor` with
your current post-processor if you want your strategy to work with
`index.search()`. Use the `default_post_processor!` macro for simple
cases:

```rust
impl DefaultPostProcessor<MyProvider, [f32]> for MyStrategy {
    default_post_processor!(Pipeline<FilterStartPoints, RemoveDeletedIdsAndCopy>);
}
```

**If you call `index.search()`** — no API change for strategies that
implement `DefaultPostProcessor` (all built-in strategies do).

**If you need custom post-processing** — use `index.search_with()` and
pass your post-processor directly:

```rust
let processor = MyCustomPostProcessor::new(/* ... */);
let stats = index.search_with(params, &strategy, processor, &context, &query, &mut output).await?;
```

**If you implement `Search`** — the method signature has changed. `PP`
and `OB` are now method-level generics, and `processor` is an explicit
parameter:

```rust
fn search<O, PP, OB>(
    self,
    index: &DiskANNIndex<DP>,
    strategy: &S,
    processor: PP,       // new
    context: &DP::Context,
    query: &T,
    output: &mut OB,
) -> impl SendFuture<ANNResult<Self::Output>>
where
    O: Send,
    PP: for<'a> SearchPostProcess<S::SearchAccessor<'a>, T, O> + Send + Sync,
    OB: SearchOutputBuffer<O> + Send + ?Sized;
```

**If you use range search results** — `RangeSearchOutput` no longer
carries `ids` and `distances` fields. Results are written to the output
buffer passed to `search()`. Use `Vec<Neighbor<u32>>` (or any
`SearchOutputBuffer` impl) to collect them.

**If you implement `InplaceDeleteStrategy`**: Add the
`DeleteSearchAccessor` associated type, ensuring it is the same as the
accessor selected by the `SearchStrategy`. This is redundant
information, but needed to help constrain downstream trait bounds.

---------

commit.message:
Retry on malformed JSON from model instead of failing immediately

Model sometimes produces invalid JSON (unescaped characters, broken
syntax). These are transient — retrying often succeeds. Removed
'parse plan' from the non-retryable error list so JSON parse errors
and missing-JSON errors trigger a retry.

commit.message:
Rewrite enemy visuals to use 3D geometries with distinct type designs

Replace box-based enemy models with rounded 3D geometries:
- Body: CylinderGeometry (rounded torso) instead of BoxGeometry
- Head: SphereGeometry instead of BoxGeometry
- Arms/Legs: CylinderGeometry instead of BoxGeometry
- All parts use MeshStandardMaterial with roughness/metalness
- Eyes glow red (normal) or yellow (bosses) via MeshBasicMaterial

Add detailed type-specific features for all 12 enemy types:
- zombie: hunched posture, bone protrusions, torn flesh, hanging jaw
- fast: fin crest, shoulder spikes, lean forward tilt
- tank: shoulder pauldrons, chest plate, rivets, belt
- exploding: emissive body, fuse+spark, glowing cracks, inner core
- ogre: tusks, brow ridge, chain, battle scars, spiked club
- fireDragon: snout, bone+membrane wings, dorsal spines, tail, glowing belly
- giant: crystal crown, rocky patches, rune lines, massive fists
- shield: round disc shield, boss emblem, helmet visor, armor trim
- jumping: large green eyes, spring-coil legs, webbed feet
- ranged: hood/cloak, staff with glowing orb, floating runes
- charger: forward horns, nose ring, armor plates, hoofed feet
- splitter: blobby body, internal bubbles, nucleus glow, drip tendrils

Preserves return object structure (bodyMesh, headMesh, lArm, rArm, lLeg, rLeg)
and shield userData.isShield for damage code compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): replace npm ci + install with single npm install for ibira.js

npm ci is structurally incompatible with this workflow: step 5 adds
ibira.js to package.json before npm ci runs, so the lock-file check
always fails on a first-time add (and on any run where the lock file
was not pre-updated). Replace both calls with a single targeted
npm install, which adds/updates ibira.js and regenerates only the
affected lock-file entries in one atomic step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Resilient tracked-path handling, --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging (#2)

* Warn instead of fail on tracked paths, add --pedantic, --quiet, and file logging

Tracked files in .worktreeinclude caused hooks to exit non-zero, breaking
Claude Code worktree creation. The default behavior now warns and skips
tracked paths (they're already in the worktree via Git). Use --pedantic
to restore the strict fail-on-tracked behavior.

Both implementations now log all output to worktree.log in the current
directory with timestamps and levels. --quiet suppresses stderr while
keeping the log file, useful for hook mode debugging.

Tests: 98 assertions (was 86), covering warn-on-tracked, --pedantic,
and --quiet for both Python and Bash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review: log file validation, test isolation, README clarity

- --quiet + unwritable log file is now fatal (both implementations),
  preventing silent output loss
- test_create_quiet runs from a dedicated temp cwd and asserts
  worktree.log exists with content
- README gotcha clarifies the tracked-path behavior changed in v0.3
  (was error, now warn+skip by default)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add compatibility tests for spherical flatbuffer serialization (#839)

Check in and run compatibility tests for the flatbuffer serialized
Spherical quantizers.

Baselines are generated using the inline test dataset in `iface.rs` and
capture the serialized representation of the quantizer as well as the
bit-patterns for compressed vectors and all pairwise distances among the
dataset.

These should cover the following:
* Bit Width: 1, 2, 4, and 8.
* Metric: `SquaredL2`, `InnerProduct` and `Cosine`.
* PreScale: None and `ReciprocalMeanNorm`

Baseline files are checked in as JSON files to
`diskann-quantization/test/generated/spherical`. They can be regenerated
by setting the `DISKANN_TEST=overwrite` environment variable.

Additionally, this adds tests for the Cosine metric to the existing
serialization tests.

Due to test failures on macos, the `Check` enum for absolute/relative
tolerance checking has been promoted to
`diskann_quantization::test_util`.

---------

commit.message:
Add delegate mode — helm as Copilot orchestrator

New 'delegate' mode where helm enriches issues with project context
(language, build commands, SOT, file tree, manifests) and assigns
them to Copilot coding agent instead of generating code directly.

- agent.mode = 'delegate' in helm.toml or --delegate flag
- Context comment with structured markdown and <details> sections
- Assigns issue to @copilot via gh issue edit
- Daemon logs warning when running in delegate mode
- Human-friendly output shows delegated status and next steps
- Direct mode (current behavior) remains the default

Copilot works inside a Codespace and can build/test/iterate,
making this suitable for complex codebases where single-shot
Models API generation falls short.

commit.message:
Fix background relaunch with paths containing spaces

- Use single string for ArgumentList instead of array (spaces in
  OneDrive path broke argument splitting)
- Rename reserved variable name

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: KeyboardPaster
repository.description: PowerShell tool that types clipboard content character by character via simulated keystrokes instead of pasting

commit.message:
Prepare 2.45.0 stable release

Add the stable 2.45.0 changelog entry that rolls up the completed RC work: HA area-assisted naming, MA runtime reloads, calmer grouped operator guidance, and advanced recovery timeline diagnostics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
Auto-retry on truncated model output with larger max_tokens

When the model's finish_reason is 'length' (output exceeded max_tokens),
automatically double max_tokens and retry instead of failing with a
confusing 'unexpected end of JSON input' error. Starts at 16K tokens,
can grow to 32K or 64K on retry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prepare 2.45.0-rc.3 release

Document the advanced recovery diagnostics and HA area naming toggle work in the changelog, and bump the RC add-on manifest version to 2.45.0-rc.3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
Refine recovery diagnostics and HA area naming

Add advanced recovery timeline views in diagnostics with richer retained history and power-user filtering, and make Home Assistant area-based bridge/adapter naming suggestions toggleable while defaulting them on in HA add-on mode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
Auto-create project board in project init

When no board number is provided during interactive init, automatically
create a new GitHub Projects v2 board with the repo name as title and
configure it with standard helm statuses (Ready, In Progress, In Review,
Done). Owner defaults to the current gh user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update store/lsm_store.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: elastickv
repository.description: Elastickv: An experimental project aimed at developing a distributed key-value store for cloud environments, similar to DynamoDB. Plans to feature Raft-based replication, dynamic node scaling, and automatic hot spot re-allocation.
